cover.title: Active Directory Security Homelab Portfolio
cover.subtitle: Windows Server 2025 | Splunk SIEM | BloodHound CE | Sysmon
cover.author: Arturs Kaufmanis

section.title: Executive Summary
section.p: This portfolio documents the design, implementation, and security hardening of a comprehensive Active Directory homelab environment. The project demonstrates enterprise-level cybersecurity skills including SIEM integration, attack path analysis, identity management, and security monitoring.
The lab was built to demonstrate practical competency in:

list.item: Active Directory deployment and security hardening
Endpoint detection using Sysmon and Splunk Add-on for Sysmon
Attack path visualization using BloodHound Community Edition
WSL2, Docker, and containerized security tools

subsection.title: PingCastle Score Progression
subsection.note: 67% improvement from baseline score of 60 to final score of 20. Remaining findings are lab limitations (single DC, BreakGlass used within 60 days).

section.title: Phase 1 - Active Directory Deployment and Baseline

subsection.title: Initial Setup
subsection.p: Windows Server 2025 was installed and configured as a Domain Controller for the ADForest.local domain. The following roles were installed:

subsection.title: Baseline PingCastle Assessment
subsection.p: PingCastle was used to establish a security baseline. Initial scan revealed a global score of 60 with multiple critical findings:

row.description: Local Admin Password Solution not deployed
Insufficient audit policy on Domain Controllers
Default quota allows any user to join computers to domain
DC subnets not declared in AD Sites and Services

subsection.title: Password Policy
subsection.p: Default password policy was strengthened to meet enterprise standards:
subsection.result: Minimum password length increased from 7 to 12 characters.

subsection.title: AD Recycle Bin
subsection.p: Enabled AD Recycle Bin to allow recovery of accidentally deleted objects:
subsection.result: Deleted AD objects can now be recovered within 180 days (tombstone lifetime).

subsection.title: MachineAccountQuota
subsection.p: By default, any domain user can join up to 10 computers to the domain. This was disabled to prevent unauthorized domain joins:
subsection.result: Only administrators can now join computers to the domain, preventing unauthorized device registration.

subsection.title: Schema Admins Cleanup
subsection.p: The built-in Administrator account was removed from Schema Admins. Schema Admins is one of the most powerful groups in AD - members can make irreversible forest-wide schema modifications:
subsection.best_practice: Schema Admins must be empty at all times except during planned schema modifications. Members are only added temporarily and removed immediately after.

subsection.title: Account Delegation Protection
subsection.p: The Administrator account was protected against Kerberos delegation attacks:
subsection.result: Prevents attackers from using delegation to impersonate the Administrator account.

subsection.title: NTLMv1 Disabled
subsection.p: NTLMv1 is a legacy and insecure authentication protocol. It was disabled via both registry and GPO:
subsection.note: LmCompatibilityLevel 5 = Only NTLMv2 responses are sent. NTLMv1 and LM are refused.

subsection.title: Audit Policy
subsection.p: Comprehensive audit policy was configured to capture all security-relevant events:

subsection.title: AD Backup
subsection.p: Windows Server Backup was installed and a system state backup was configured:
subsection.command: diskpart /s backup_script.txt # Creates 30GB VHD mounted as E:
subsection.result: System state backup completed successfully, covering AD database, SYSVOL, and registry.

subsection.title: AES Encryption
subsection.p: Legacy RC4 Kerberos encryption was replaced with modern AES encryption:
subsection.command: # Enable AES for all user accounts Get-ADUser -Filter {Enabled -eq $true} | Set-ADUser -KerberosEncryptionType AES128,AES256
# Enable AES for krbtgt account Set-ADUser -Identity krbtgt -KerberosEncryptionType AES128,AES256
subsection.result: All Kerberos tickets now use AES256 encryption, eliminating RC4 downgrade attack vectors.

section.title: Phase 3 - LAPS Implementation
section.p: Windows LAPS (Local Administrator Password Solution) is built into Windows Server 2025. It automatically manages and rotates local administrator passwords on domain-joined computers, eliminating the risk of password reuse attacks.

subsection.title: LAPS Schema Extension
subsection.p: AD Schema was extended with LAPS attributes:
subsection.command: # Add Schema Admins temporarily for schema modification Add-ADGroupMember -Identity 'Schema Admins' -Members 'SecAdmin'
# Extend schema with LAPS attributes Update-LapsADSchema -Verbose
subsection.p: Schema attributes added:
subsection.command: # Remove from Schema Admins immediately after Remove-ADGroupMember -Identity 'Schema Admins' -Members 'SecAdmin' -Confirm:$false

subsection.title: LAPS Computer Permissions
subsection.result: Computers can now update their own LAPS password attribute in AD.

subsection.title: LAPS GPO Configuration
subsection.command: # Backup passwords to Active Directory Set-GPRegistryValue -Name 'LAPS Policy' -Key 'HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\LAPS' -ValueName 'BackupDirectory' -Type DWord -Value 2

# Password age 30 days, minimum length 16 characters Set-GPRegistryValue -Name 'LAPS Policy' -Key 'HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\LAPS' -ValueName 'PasswordAgeDays' -Type DWord -Value 30 Set-GPRegistryValue -Name 'LAPS Policy' -Key 'HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\LAPS' -ValueName 'PasswordLength' -Type DWord -Value 16
subsection.impact: A-LAPS-Not-Installed finding resolved. PingCastle score reduced by 15 points.

subsection.title: SecAdmin Account Creation
subsection.p: A dedicated administrative account was created following the principle of least privilege. The built-in Administrator account (RID-500) is a well-known attack target - creating a separate admin account reduces this risk:
subsection.command: # Mark as sensitive - cannot be delegated Set-ADUser -Identity SecAdmin -AccountNotDelegated $true

subsection.title: BreakGlass Account (Emergency Recovery)
subsection.p: The built-in Administrator account was renamed to BreakGlass and disabled. This follows defence-in-depth principles - attackers expect 'Administrator' but find 'BreakGlass' disabled:
subsection.command: # Rename Administrator to BreakGlass Rename-ADObject -Identity (Get-ADUser Administrator).DistinguishedName -NewName 'BreakGlass'
subsection.note: Security Principles: Least Privilege, Defence in Depth, Security by Obscurity. The BreakGlass account password is stored securely offline.

section.title: Phase 5 - Splunk SIEM Integration
section.p: Splunk Enterprise 10.2 was deployed as the SIEM solution. Despite being the free version (500MB/day ingestion limit), it provides full SIEM functionality for the homelab environment.

subsection.title: Splunk Configuration
subsection.p: Windows Event Log collection was configured via inputs.conf (GUI editing restricted in free version):
subsection.command:
[WinEventLog://Security] index = wineventlog disabled = false start_from = oldest current_only = 1 renderXml = false
[WinEventLog://System] index = wineventlog disabled = false current_only = 1
[WinEventLog://Application] index = wineventlog disabled = false current_only = 1
[WinEventLog://Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational] index = wineventlog disabled = false current_only = 1 renderXml = true whitelist = 1,3,8,10,11,12,13,22

subsection.title: Sysmon Integration
subsection.p: Sysmon (System Monitor) v15.15 was installed and integrated with Splunk for deep endpoint visibility. Key challenge: Splunk returned errorCode=5 (Access Denied) when accessing Sysmon logs.
Resolution: Splunk service was configured to run as LocalSystem:
Splunk Add-on for Sysmon (TA-microsoft-sysmon v5.0.0) was installed to enable proper field extraction and sourcetype recognition.

subsection.title: Data Collection Results

row.events: Failed logons, privilege use, group changes

subsection.note: Total daily ingestion: ~253MB - well within the 500MB free version limit. Sysmon filtered to critical event IDs: 1, 3, 8, 10, 11, 12, 13, 22.

row.query: index=wineventlog EventCode=4625 | stats count by Account_Name | sort -count
index=wineventlog | stats count by EventCode | sort -count | head 10
index=wineventlog sourcetype=xmlwineventlog | stats count by Image | sort -count | head 10
index=wineventlog EventCode=4624 | stats count by Account_Name | sort -count

list.item: AD Security Overview - Top Event Codes bar chart

section.title: Phase 6 - BloodHound CE Attack Path Analysis
section.p: BloodHound Community Edition was deployed to visualize Active Directory attack paths - a critical tool for both offensive security (red team) and defensive security (blue team) operations.

row.details: WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04 LTS on Windows Server 2025

subsection.title: Deployment Process
subsection.p: Key challenge: Docker Desktop does not support Windows Server. Solution: WSL2 with Docker Engine on Ubuntu.
subsection.command: # Install Docker Engine on Ubuntu sudo apt-get install -y docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-compose-plugin
# Deploy BloodHound CE wget -O docker-compose.yml 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SpecterOps/BloodHound/main/examples/docker-compose/docker-compose.yml' docker compose up -d

subsection.title: SharpHound Data Collection
subsection.result: 328 AD objects enumerated including users, groups, computers, GPOs, ACLs, and trust relationships.

subsection.title: Saved BloodHound Queries
subsection.note: These queries are saved in BloodHound CE for reuse across future assessments and demonstrate proficiency in Cypher query language.

row.root_cause: Splunk service lacked permission to read Sysmon event log
row.resolution: Changed Splunk service to run as LocalSystem via sc.exe config
Used robocopy to copy correct inner folder to Splunk apps directory

row.root_cause: Docker Desktop requires Windows 10/11 desktop OS
row.resolution: Installed WSL2 + Ubuntu + Docker Engine as alternative

row.root_cause: v8.7.0 does not include Linux CLI binary
Schema Admin group membership requires new login session
row.resolution: Restarted Windows Server to refresh Kerberos token

row.root_cause: SecAdmin left in Schema Admins after LAPS update
row.resolution: Removed SecAdmin from Schema Admins - best practice

row.root_cause: Stats queries show in Statistics tab not Events tab
row.resolution: Clicked Statistics tab to view results

row.root_cause: Windows Defender flags SharpHound as security tool
row.resolution: Temporarily disabled real-time monitoring during download

subsection.title: TopTon Security Gateway (In Progress)
subsection.p: A TopTon mini PC (Intel N150, 33GB DDR5) is being configured as an inline security gateway:
Software stack:

list.item: BadBlood - Realistic AD test data population
Wazuh SIEM - Open source alternative/complement to Splunk
